crochetPattern.name: Bag of Holding
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
gauge: 16 dc and 10 dc rows = 4 in. / 10 cm
Make sure to check your gauge.
notes: This pattern is worked in the intarsia style. In intarsia, you do NOT work over the nonworking color; each color section will be attached to its own yarn bobbin or ball of yarn.
This pattern can be worked by following the chart or using the written instructions.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: (Coffee) ch6, working back, sc into 2nd ch from hook (counts as first sc) 5sc (5)

items: With Coffee, sc the Side Strip to the side, bottom, and side of the Back Panel. Repeat this process: sc the other edge of the Side Strip to the side, bottom, and side of the Front Panel.
With Coffee, sc the top of the Front Flap Panel to the top of the Back Panel.
With Coffee, sc around the remaining 3 sides of the Front Flap Panel.
With Coffee, attach the rings to the top corners of the Side Strip by working 5sc through each ring. Fasten off and weave in any ends. Clip the adjustable cross-body strap to the rings.

crochetPattern.name: Beholder
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)

items.name: Eye Colors:
entries: Slime, Pale Yellow, Cyan, Varsity Orange, Magenta, small amounts

notions: 12-gauge armature wire (copper wire from a hardware store works great), 17 feet / 5 m
gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.
notes: This pattern is worked in continuous rounds unless otherwise noted.

items: {@b (MAKE 10)}
Cut 20-in. / 51-cm lengths of wire for eyestalks.

Using the needle-nose pliers, curl one end in on itself to protect yourself from injury. Then curl the end in a small spiral (use about 2 in. / 5 cm of wire to make the curl) to fit in the top of the Eyestalk at the eyeball.
{@b (MAKE 10, 2 IN EACH EYE COLOR)}
With {@b Eye Color}, make a magic ring.
Change to {@b A}.
Change to {@b MC}.

items.name: Rnd 7:
entries: Sc around in both loops of the current round as well as the unworked blo from Rnd 4. (18)

items: At this point, attach a 12-mm safety eye in the magic ring and secure.
In the following rounds, add the armature wire and polyester fiberfill as you go.

Place the spiral of the wire against the back of the eye. It helps to use the tails from the color changes to tie the wire spiral into place. Stuff firmly to keep the wire in place and continue to stuff firmly as you work.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail. You should have about 3 in. / 8 cm of wire showing from the end of the Eyestalk.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Flatten the Eyelid into a half circle. Slst the opening closed. Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing. Set aside until you are finished with the Head/Body.
With {@b B}, make a magic ring.
Pull up a loop, bring {@b B} forward, do not cut off. Attach {@b A} in next st.
Pull up a loop, send {@b A} to the back and put your hook into {@b B}.
Slst with {@b B} in the spaces between {@b B} and {@b A} all the way around. (60)
Fasten off {@b B}.

items.name: Rnd 13:
entries: Pick {@b A} up again, 5sc, inc, [10sc, inc] × 5, 5sc (72)

items: Place the 24-mm safety eye in the center of the magic ring and secure into place.
Change to {@b MC}.

items.name: Rnd 22:
entries: Work in both the regular loops as well as the unworked blo from Rnd 19, 7sc, inc, [14sc, inc] × 5, 7sc. (96)

items: Now we will be making the Mouth opening.

items.name: Rnd 27:
entries: [19sc, inc] × 2, 8sc, ch1, turn, dec, 4sc, dec, ch1, turn, dec, 2sc, dec. Fasten off.

items: Sk24, attach yarn, 8sc, ch1, turn, dec, 4sc, dec, ch1, turn, dec, 2sc, dec, 2sc evenly down the side of the cheek you just made, make dec in the base of the cheek and the next st, 18sc, inc, 19sc, inc (118 visible sc stitches)

items.name: Rnd 28:
entries: 10sc, inc, 20sc, inc, 10sc, 2sc evenly up the side of the cheek, 4sc across the top of the cheek, ch38, skip the Mouth, begin at the top of the other cheek, 16sc, inc, 20sc, inc, 10sc (138)

items: Right now we are going to pause here so we can work on the Mouth. Insert a stitch marker in the loop to save your place.
With {@b MC}, we are now going to work on the inside of the Mouth.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Attach yarn to the bottom corner of the Mouth. 38sc across the bottom of the Mouth, 4sc up the cheek, 24sc across the top of the Mouth, 4sc down the other cheek. (70)

items.name: Rnd 4:
entries: In regular loops and unworked blo from Rnd 2, 38sc, 2dec, 24sc, 2dec (66)

items: Change to C.
Slst, fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
Push the Mouth to the inside of the Head/Body and squeeze the inside of the Mouth closed so that it is evenly flat across the Mouth. From the inside of the Head/Body, sew the Mouth closed.
Now make the Teeth.
With {@b A}, attach yarn in a space between the {@b MC} and {@b C}.
[Ch4, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, 2slst, sc, sk st in the Mouth, 2slst] repeat until you have worked all the way around the Mouth.
Fasten off, bring yarn tails to the inside of the Head/Body, and weave in ends.
{@b (CONTINUED)}
Now that the Mouth is complete, let's finish the Head/Body. Remove stitch marker and continue as follows.
Place a stitch marker in your loop. We will now attach 5 of the Eyestalks.

Get 1 Eyestalk in each color. Eyestalks will go somewhere between Rnds 39 and 55. Place 1 Eyestalk above the main eye, slightly off center. Poke the wire into the Head/Body and use your pliers to curl the end of the wire and bend it flush with the inside of the Head/Body. Pin the Eyestalk in place and sew. Be sure to stuff the base of the Eyestalk completely as you sew it in place.

Continue placing the next 4 Eyestalks in a kind of headband, but do not place any below the corners of the Mouth. They do not need to be equidistant from each other. Once the first 5 Eyestalks are in place, curl them toward the face to make it easier to continue working on the rest of the Head/Body.

Place a stitch marker in your loop. We will now attach the rest of the Eyestalks. Attach the Eyestalks in the same manner as before, placing them behind and offset from the first 5 stalks. Try not to place 2 of the same color eyes next to each other. Curl the Eyestalks toward the face and continue working on the rest of the Head/Body.

Begin to stuff by placing the dried beans in a nylon stocking or another fabric bag and place in the bottom of the Head/Body. Stuff firmly with the polyester fiberfill to hold the bag in place. Be sure to stuff around the Mouth and push the polyester fiberfill around until you fill the area well. Stuff the eye very firmly so it can keep its shape. Continue to stuff firmly as you close up the Head/Body.
Fasten off, sew opening closed, and weave in ends.

Now attach the Eyelid. Pin the slst edge along the top of the main eye, just to the outside of the ridge we made in {@b MC}. Sew into place. If you like, you can add a bit of hot glue under the Eyelid to hold it in place.

crochetPattern.name: Bracers of Archery
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
notions: 10 metal eyelets, ¾ in. / 2 cm in diameter (optional)
gauge: 20 dc and 12 dc rows = 4 in. / 10 cm
Make sure to check your gauge.
notes: This pattern uses the tapestry crochet technique. With tapestry technique, you change colors as indicated, carrying the unused color inside the stitches throughout the row.
This pattern can be worked by following the chart or using the written instructions.
All stitches in this pattern are single crochet.

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: (Coffee) Ch18, working back, sc into 2nd ch from hook (counts as first sc), 16sc (17)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: (Coffee) 6sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 6sc (17)

entries: (Coffee) 6sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 6sc (17)

entries: (Coffee) inc sc, (Café Latte) 2sc, (Coffee) 5sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 5sc, (Cafe Latte) 2sc, (Coffee) inc sc (19)

items.name: Row 11:
entries: (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) 3sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 3sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 3sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) 3sc, (Coffee) sc (19)

items.name: Row 18:

items.name: Row 20:
entries: (Coffee) 2sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) 2sc, (Coffee) 3sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 3sc, (Café Latte) 2sc, (Coffee) sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 2sc (19)

items.name: Row 23:
entries: (Coffee) inc sc, (Café Latte) 4sc, (Coffee) 3sc, (Café Latte) sc, (Coffee) 3sc, (Café Latte) 4sc, (Coffee) inc sc (21)

items: {@b (MAKE 2 BORDERS ON EACH PANEL)}
With Café Latte, attach yarn on the side of Row 1 of Center Panel. Work a Border along the side of Rows 1–31 of Center Panel.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch1 (does not count as first sc), 31sc across, working 1sc into the side of each row

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch2 (counts as first hdc), 2hdc. [Ch1, sk st, 5hdc] × 4. Ch1, sk st, 3hdc. (31)

items.name: Row 3:
entries: Ch1, 31sc across. Fasten off and weave in any ends.

items: Repeat to make a Border on the other side of the Center Panel.

items.name: Optional:
entries: Install metal eyelets into the spaces created in Row 2 of the borders. Otherwise, use lacing in the existing spaces to lace up the Bracer.

crochetPattern.name: D20 Pillow

yarn.name: Large Pillow
entries: Super bulky weight (#6 super bulky) yarn, shown in Bernat {@i Blanket Yarn} (100% polyester, 220 yd. / 201 m per 10½ oz. / 300 g skein)

yarn.name: Small Pillow
entries: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing doesn't show through.
notes: Each Triangle is crocheted from the top down, using the tapestry crochet technique. When referencing the pattern charts, start from the top of the image and work your way down.
Nineteen of the Triangles have a Number crocheted on them. The twentieth Triangle has the D&D Ampersand.
The patterns are worked following the charts, changing colors as you go, and the finished Triangles are stitched together following the diagrams.

items: {@b (MAKE 20)}

The body of the Triangle is made with {@b A} and the Numbers and Ampersand symbol are made with {@b B}. Follow the charts on {@footnote this page|{@note See the associated images.}} for each Triangle.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch2, turn and 2sc in 2nd ch from the hook, ch1 and turn (2)

items.name: Row 23:
entries: 27sc, [sc, ch1, sc] in the last stitch in the row. Do not turn. (29)

items.name: Row 24:
entries: Working up the right side of the Triangle, 20sc, inc. In the top point of the Triangle [sc, ch1, sc] in the same stitch. Working down the left side of the Triangle, inc, 20sc. In the left corner of the Triangle sc in the last available space (the first sc in Row 23) and fasten off with an invisible finish in the first stitch of Row 23 (this creates the sc, ch1, sc corner stitch similar to the other Triangle points). Fasten off with a short yarn tail. The completed Triangle should have 76 stitches (not including the ch1 spaces).

entries.name: Part 1: Middle Section
items: Attach each Triangle with the front facing outward. Triangles are attached with single crochet stitches in the back loops of the stitches around the edge of the Triangles. The first stitch to attach the Triangles is made in the 2nd sc in the "sc, ch1, sc" corner stitches. Do not crochet in the corner chain spaces. Each Triangle has 24 stitches on the sides and 28 stitches on the bottom.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Starting with the "17" and "3" Triangles—lay both Triangles together with their top points aligned and the front sides facing outward. Sc blo in each stitch up the right side of the "17" and the left side of the "3" to connect the two Triangles.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Attach the right side of the "16" Triangle to the right side of the "3" Triangle (the "16" should be pointing downward). Continue making sc blo stitches to connect the sides of the two Triangles.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Attach the right side of the "6" Triangle to the left side of the "16" Triangle, using the same process as in Step 2.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Attach the right side of the "14" Triangle to the bottom of the "6" Triangle. 6sc blo (includes starting sc to attach the Triangles), inc blo by working in the same space as the last sc blo on the "14" and the next space on the "6." Repeat across.

items.name: Step 5:
entries: Attach the bottom of the "4" Triangle to the left side of the "14" Triangle, using the same process as in Step 4.

items.name: Step 6:
entries: Attach the right side of the "18" Triangle to the left side of the "4" Triangle, using the same process as in Step 2.

items.name: Step 7:
entries: Attach the left side of the "5" Triangle to the left side of the "18" Triangle, using the same process as in Step 2.

items.name: Step 8:
entries: Attach the left side of the "15" Triangle to the right side of the "5" Triangle, using the same process in Step 2.

items.name: Step 9:
entries: Attach the left side of the "7" Triangle to the bottom of the "15" Triangle. 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the next space on the "15" and the same space as the last sc blo on the "7." Repeat across. Fasten off with a short yarn tail.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Starting with the "20" Triangle (D&D logo) and the "2" Triangle—lay both Triangles with the right side of the "20" and the bottom of the "2" together and the front sides facing outward. Starting in the bottom right corner of the "20," 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "20" and the next stitch on the "2." Repeat across.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Attach the bottom of the "8" Triangle to the left side of the "20" Triangle, following the same process as in Step 1. Fasten off with a short yarn tail.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Attach the right side of the "8" Triangle to the bottom of the "10" Triangle. 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the next stitch on the "10" and the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "8." Repeat across.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Attach the bottom of the "12" Triangle to the left side of the "2" Triangle, using the same process as in Step 3. Fasten off with a short yarn tail

items.name: Step 5:
entries: Attach the left side of the "12" Triangle to the right side of the "10" Triangle with a sc blo at the top point of both Triangles. Sc blo in each stitch across. Fasten off with a long yarn tail. Using a tapestry needle and the yarn tail end, sew together the points of all 5 Triangles where they connect at the top. Fasten off and weave in the end.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Starting with the "11" Triangle and the "9" Triangle—lay both Triangles with the left side of the "11" and the right side of the "9" together. Starting on the top left side of the "11," sc blo in each stitch across.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Attach the left side of the "13" Triangle to the bottom side of the "11" Triangle. 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the next stitch on the "11" and the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "13." Repeat across. Fasten off with a short yarn tail.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Attach the bottom of the "1" Triangle to the bottom of the "13" Triangle. Sc blo in each stitch across.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Attach the right side of the "1" Triangle to the bottom side of the "19" Triangle. 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "1" and the next stitch on the "19." Repeat across. Fasten off with a short yarn tail.

items.name: Step 5:
entries: Attach the right side of the "19" Triangle to the bottom side of the "9" Triangle, starting in the bottom left corner of the "9." 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the next stitch on the "9" and the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "19." Repeat across. Fasten off with a long yarn tail. Using a tapestry needle and the yarn tail end, sew together the points of all 5 Triangles where they connect at the top. Fasten off and weave in the end.

entries.name: Part 4: Attaching The Sections

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Align the left side of the "17" Triangle with the left side of the "10" Triangle. Starting in the top left corner of the "10," sc blo in each stitch across. 1sc blo in the top of the "3" and the corner where the "8" and "10" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Working across the left side of the "8" and the bottom side of the "16" Triangles, 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "8" and the next stitch on the "16." Repeat across. 1sc blo in the corner of the "6" and the corner where the "8" and "20" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Working across the bottom side of the "20" and "14" Triangles, sc blo in each stitch across. 1sc blo in the corner of the "4" and the corner where the "20" and "2" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Working across the right side of the "2" and the bottom side of the "18" Triangles, 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "2" and the next stitch on the "18." Repeat across. 1sc blo in the top of the "5" and the corner where the "2" and "12" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 5:
entries: Working across the right side of the "12" and the right side of the "15" Triangles, sc blo in each stitch across. 1sc blo in the top of the "7" and the corner where the "12" and "10" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 6:
entries: Working down the right side of the "7" and the bottom side of the "17" Triangles, 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "7" and the next stitch on the "17." Repeat across. 1sc blo in the top of the "1" and the corner where the "19" and "3" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 7:
entries: Align the bottom side of the "7" and the left side of the "1" Triangles. 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "1" and the next stitch on the "7." Repeat across. 1sc blo in the corner of the "15" and the corner where the "1" and "13" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 8:
entries: Working across the right side of the "13" and the bottom side of the "5" Triangles, 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "13" and the next stitch on the "5." Repeat across. 1sc blo in the top of the "18" and the corner where the "13" and "11" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 9:
entries: Working across the right side of the "11" and the right side of the "4" Triangles, sc blo in each stitch across. 1sc blo in the top of the "14" and the corner where the "11" and the "9" Triangles connect.

items: {@i Note: Before moving on to the final two steps, stuff the pillow medium firm with polyester fiberfill.}

items.name: Step 10:
entries: Working across the left side of the "9" and the left side of the "6" Triangles, sc blo in each stitch across. 1sc blo in the top of the "16" and in the corner where the "9" and "19" Triangles connect.

items.name: Step 11:
entries: Working across the left side of the "19" and the bottom side of the "3" Triangles, 6sc blo, inc blo by working in the same stitch as the last sc blo on the "19" and the next stitch on the "3." Repeat across. 1sc blo in the corner of the "17" and the corner where the "19" and "1" Triangles connect. Fasten off and weave in any ends.

items: All color changes start from the top leftmost side of the chart.
The color change for number 1 starts in Row 11, stitch 8.
The color changes for numbers 2, 3, 5, 6, 8, and 9 start in Row 11, stitch 7.
The color changes for numbers 4 and 7 start in Row 11, stitch 6.
The color changes for numbers 10 through 19 start in Row 11, stitch 4.
The color change for the D&D Ampersand starts in Row 5, right after the inc in the first stitch of the row.

crochetPattern.name: Displacer Beast
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value Pound Plus} (100% acrylic, 1093 yd. / 1000 m per 17.6 oz / 500 g skein)

items.name: Additional Colors:
entries: Small amounts of worsted-weight (#4 medium) yarn in Pink, Cream, and Black

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.

items.name: Puff Stitch (puff):
entries: Yarn over, insert hook and pull up a loop 5 times, yo and pull through all loops on hook and ch1. To work the next row above the puff, work in the ch1 space.

items.name: Double Puff Stitch (dblpuff):
entries: Yarn over, insert hook into first space and pull up a loop 2 times, yo and insert hook into next space 3 times, yo and insert hook into next space 2 times, yo and pull through all loops on the hook, and ch1. To work the next row above the dblpuff, work in the ch1 space. You are decreasing when you make this stitch.

items.name: Popcorn Stitch (popcorn):
entries: 5dc in stitch indicated, remove hook from loop, insert hook into first dc made, grab loop and pull through, ch1 to finish. When working in the top of the popcorn, work in the ch1.

notes: Each popcorn stitch (see above) is considered one stitch for the total stitch count at the end of each round. Carry the colors with you instead of cutting at every color change.

items: With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Sc, slst, fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Insert safety eyes between Rnds 21 and 22, 15 spaces apart between each eye post.
Begin stuffing the Head firmly and continue stuffing as you go.
Fasten off, sew hole closed, and weave in the tail.
Pin Muzzle to the Head—the bridge of the nose will be in between the eyes. Stuff as you pin. Sew in place and stuff firmly as you sew.
With {@b Black}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch5, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, 4sc (4)

items: Fasten off, leave a long tail for sewing.
Pin the Nose to the Muzzle. Sew into place.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch13, beginning in 2nd ch from hook sc, hdc, 8dc, hdc, sc, ch13, beginning in 2nd ch from hook sc, hdc, 8dc, hdc, sc (24)

items: This makes 2 Eyelids that are connected.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail.
Pin the points and flat edge around the eyes first, then pull the edges to open the Eyelids around the eyes. Sew into place.

entries.name: Outside Ears
items: With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, 3sc in next, sc (5)

items: Fasten off, weave in ends.

entries.name: Inside Ears
items: With {@b Pink}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, 3sc in next, sc (5)

items: With {@b MC}

items.name: Row 11:
entries: Ch1, turn, stack the Inside Ear on top of the Outside Ear, working through both pieces, 10sc, 3sc in next, 10sc (23)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Ch1 and work 20sc evenly across the bottom of the Ear, slst (20)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing. Weave in all other ends.
Curve the Ears and pin to the top of the Head. Sew in place.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch7, beginning in 2nd ch from hook 5sc, 2sc in last ch, working on the backside of the ch, 5sc (12)

items: Now you will work in rows.

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Ch1, turn, dec, 5sc, dec, sc in the side of Row 11, dec on Rnd 10, 5sc, dec, sc in the side of Row 11 (16)

items: Now the first st of the round is the first dec from Row 12.
Stuff the Foot and Leg as you work.

As you finish the top of the Leg around the shoulder don't stuff it quite so full. It needs to be somewhat flattened when it gets attached to the Body. However, the rest of the Leg still needs to be stuffed firmly.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail. Sew hole closed, leaving the tail so you can attach the Leg to the Body.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch7, beginning in 2nd ch from hook 5sc, 2sc in last ch, working on the backside of the ch, 5sc (12)

items: Now you will work in rows.

items.name: Row 10:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items.name: Row 11:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items.name: Row 13:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items: Now you will work in rounds.
Stuff the Foot and Leg as you work.

As you finish the top of the Leg around the shoulder, don't stuff it quite so full. It needs to be somewhat flattened when it gets attached to the Body. However, the rest of the Leg still needs to be stuffed firmly.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail. Sew hole closed, leaving the tail so you can attach the Leg to the Body.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch7, beginning in 2nd ch from hook 5sc, 2sc in last ch, working on the backside of the ch, 5sc (12)

items: Begin stuffing, and continue stuffing as you go.
Fasten off and sew hole closed, leaving a long tail for sewing.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Mark the center decrease with a stitch marker.
Mark the center decrease with a stitch marker.
The side with the three decreases is the front of the Haunch—basically, the knee.
Stuff lightly, you will be flattening the Haunch against the Body.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail.

Pin the Haunch to the Back Foot/Leg with the knee facing toward the toes and the back of the Haunch lined up with the back of the Foot. The bottom of the Haunch should be slightly opened so it can be sewn in an oval shape to the top of the Foot. Use the tail from the Foot to sew the Haunch into place. Save the tail from the Haunch to sew the Back Foot/Leg to the Body.

instructions.name: Tentacles
entries: Cut two 25-in. / 64-cm lengths of wire. With pliers bend ½ in. / 1 cm of one end over to keep the point from poking through the material. Now, make a complete bend 5 in. / 12.5 cm from the same end, forming a really long U-shape. Curve the 2 sides of the U outward to make a petal-like shape for the Tentacle Heads. Continue to adjust these as you assemble your Tentacle Heads.

entries.name: Inner Tentacle Head
items: With {@b Pink}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, sc across (3)

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc (3)

items.name: Row 4:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc, switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc (5)

items.name: Row 6:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, [switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc] × 3 (7)

items: Now you can cut the {@b Cream} color.

items.name: Row 18:
entries: Ch1, turn, 2sc, 3sc in last st, turn and 15sc evenly down the side, (3sc, sc, 3sc) along the bottom, 15sc evenly up the side, 2sc in same st as first sc of this row, slst (44)

items: Fasten off, weave in ends.

entries.name: Outer Tentacle Head
items: With {@b MC}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, sc across (3)

items.name: Row 18:
entries: Ch1, turn, 2sc, 3sc in last st, turn and 15sc evenly down the side, (3sc, sc, 3sc) along the bottom, 15sc evenly up the side, 2sc in same st as first sc of this row, slst (44)

items: Do not fasten off.

entries.name: Assembling Tentacles
items: Sandwich the wrong sides of the Inner and Outer Tentacle Heads together. Make sure the tops of each piece match up. Use stitch markers to hold everything together while you work around both pieces. Do NOT place the wire yet.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch1, turn, 43sc evenly around both Heads, stuffing as you work (43; you should have 2 stitches left unworked)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing. Lay the wire in place around the Tentacle Heads and with pliers shape it to fit. Fold the flap you just made over the wire and sew into place along the seam where {@b Pink} meets {@b MC}. When you get to the end, sew the two sides together with one little stitch. You've now created the head piece for the end of the stalk of the Tentacle. Fasten off, weave in ends.

entries.name: Tentacle Stem

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Leave a long beginning tail and attach {@b MC} anywhere along the bottom of the Tentacle Head. 12sc evenly around the {@b MC} part only (do not work in the Pink part). Leave the long tail hanging out in case you need to stitch up any gaping holes. (12)

items: Stuff and continue stuffing as you work.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
Cut an 18-in. / 46-cm length of wire. Using pliers, bend about ½ in. / 1 cm over on itself to keep the end from poking through the tip of the Tail.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Insert the bent end of the wire into the start of the Tail and stuff around it as you work. Use the yarn tail to hold the wire in place.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Place a stitch marker in the loop.

items.name: Attach the Tail to the Body:
entries: Poke the wire of the Tail between Rnds 8 and 9, directly below the first st of the round. Using pliers, wrap the end of the wire into a spiral, then fold flat against the fabric inside the Body. Pin the Tail, and sew in place. This will make the Body lean slightly forward. This is fine; the Legs will stabilize the Body once you attach them. Feel free to curl the Tail up into an S-shape. This will help keep it out of the way while you work.

items: Begin stuffing the Body firmly.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.

items.name: Attach the Tentacles:
entries: First determine where the center of the back of the neck is, using the center of the Tail as a reference point. Insert a stitch marker to identify the area. Insert the wire from the Tentacles between Rnds 63 and 54, with about 7 stitches between the wires. Using pliers, curl the wire into a spiral and bend the spiral to flatten against the fabric inside the Body. Pin the Tentacles in place, making sure that the Tentacle Heads face toward the neck opening, and sew. Stuff the ends of the Tentacles completely as you sew.

items: Finish stuffing the Body firmly, as much as possible, up to the neck.

items.name: Back Legs:
entries: Brace the Body in an upright position, leaning forward so that the Tail isn't squished under the Body. Press the Haunch slightly into the foot so that it leans forward just a bit. Pin the Back Legs onto the Body with the back of the Haunches flattened completely from the back of the foot up to the beginning round, one or two stitches away from either side of the Tail. Leave the front of the Haunches unpinned. Press the Back Legs toward the Body and pin in place. This will help keep the Body from tipping forward. Use the yarn tail from the back of the foot to sew the Haunch and Back Legs in place.

items.name: Middle Legs:
entries: With the Body standing on the Back Legs, press the Middle Legs into the floor and against the back foot. There should be as little space as possible between the two. Press the top of the Middle Leg flat against the Body and pin in place. Sew the Middle Leg around the shoulder and tack the inner part of the Middle Leg to the Body to keep it from flaring out.

items.name: Front Legs:
entries: Pin the Front Legs just above the Middle Legs, with the shoulder completely flattened against the Body. Sew into place, only around the back curve of the shoulder, with each foot in a slightly different raised position to give the appearance that the beast is about to jump after its Tentacles. If the foot wants to flare out, you can tack it to the Body with a hidden stitch or two.

items.name: Attach the Head to the Body:
entries: Make sure to stuff the neck well as you sew the Body to the Head to keep it from flopping forward.

crochetPattern.name: Eye and Hand of Vecna
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Crow & Crescent {@i Rhea Worsted} (100% merino wool, 218 yd. / 199 m per 3½ oz. / 100 g skein)
Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn of your choice

items.name: Color C (iris):
entries: Pale Blue or Green, 2 yd. / 1.8 m

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.
notes: This pattern uses a consistent yarn weight and can be made with many different types of yarn. Light to medium yarn (DK or worsted weight) produces the most realistically sized hand.

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b A}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a short tail to weave in or tuck inside the finger. Do NOT stuff. Mark the 5th stitch of the last row and set aside.
With {@b A}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a short tail to weave in or tuck inside. Do NOT stuff. Mark the 5th stitch in the last row and set aside.
With {@b A}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a short tail to weave or tuck inside the finger. Do NOT stuff. Mark the 8th stitch in the last row and set aside.
With {@b A}, make a magic ring.
Do NOT fasten off or move stitch marker. Continue to Palm and Wrist.
With {@b A}, continue where you left off on the Pinky Finger.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch2, starting in the marked stitch on the Ring finger, 7sc, ch2, starting in the marked stitch on the Middle Finger, 7sc, ch2, starting in the marked stitch on the Index Finger, 12sc, 2sc into chain, 5sc, 2sc into chain, 5sc, 2sc into chain, 2sc (44)

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: 3sc, [hdc inc] × 4, 5sc, [hdc inc] × 4, 5sc, [hdc inc] × 4, 5sc, [hdc inc] × 4, 6sc, dec, 5sc, dec, 5sc, dec, 2sc (71)

items.name: Rnd 4:
entries: 6sc, [hdc dec] × 3, dec, 3sc, [hdc dec] × 3, dec, 3sc, [hdc dec] × 3, dec, 4sc, [hdc dec] × 3, dec, 4sc, dec, 5sc, dec, 4sc, dec, sc (49)

items.name: Rnd 5:
entries: 9sc, dec, 6sc, dec, 6sc, dec, 8sc, dec, 4sc, dec, 4sc, dec (43)

items.name: Rnd 6:
entries: 9sc, dec, 4sc, dec, 5sc, dec, 8sc, dec, 3sc, dec, 2sc, dec (37)

items.name: Rnd 8:
entries: Dec, 3sc, dec, 2inc, 3sc, 2inc, dec, sc, 2inc, dec, sc, 2inc, 5sc, dec, 2sc, dec, 2sc (39)

items.name: Rnd 10:
entries: 2sc, 2dec, [2inc, 2dec] × 3, 2inc, dec, inserting the hook through the {@i inside} of the marked stitch on the Thumb and into the Palm, 3sc through both pieces, then into the Palm only, 3sc, dec, 2sc (36)

items: Take four 9-in. / 23-cm pieces and one 6-in. / 15-cm piece of floral wire. Bend over the tip of each one and secure it with duct tape or floral tape so that the end doesn't poke out the tip of the finger. Insert one 9-in. / 23-cm piece of wire into the Index Finger, the Middle Finger, the Ring Finger, and the Pinky Finger. Insert the shorter wire into the Thumb.
Stuff each digit {@i lightly}, using a chopstick or another long, thin tool to push polyester fiberfill into knuckles to keep their shape. The rest of the digit does not need much stuffing, if any.
Use tape to bind the five wires together inside the Palm. Be sure to bend the ends of the wire so they do not poke out. Then stuff more firmly through the Palm and Wrist as you go.

items.name: Rnd 11:
entries: 27sc, 11sc around the outside of the Thumb, 6sc (44)

items: Finish stuffing the Palm and the Wrist and fasten off, leaving a short tail to close before weaving in the ends. If desired, glue on the fake fingernails at this stage.
With {@b D}, make a magic ring.
Change to {@b C}.
Change to {@b B}.
Stuff firmly. Fasten off, leaving a medium tail. Close the opening, then add a small highlight stitch to the edge of the pupil.
With {@b E}, ch10 and fasten off, leaving a long tail. Use the tail to sew the chain to the back of the Eye, then stitch small branching lines radiating away from the chain to replicate veins.

crochetPattern.name: Gelatinous Cube
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)
Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.
notes: The body is primarily worked in continuous rounds. The Arrow, Sword, Skull, Eyes, and Eyebrows are worked separately and then attached.
Stitches in parentheses are all worked in the same space.

items: With {@b A}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 9:
entries: 14hdc, 3hdc in corner space, [15hdc, 3hdc in corner space] × 3; slst in the next stitch and ch1 (71)

items.name: Rnd 10:
entries: Starting in the same stitch as the slst made at the end of Rnd 9, all in blo—3sc, 2dc, hdc, 3sc, dc, tr, dc, hdc, 4sc

items: (Ch3, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc), 3sc, hdc, dc, hdc, 5sc
(Ch5, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 2sc), 2sc
(Ch3, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc), 2sc, hdc, dc, hdc, 3sc, 2dc, hdc, 4sc
(Ch3, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc), 5sc
(Ch7, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 4sc), 4sc, dc, 2tr, dc, hdc, 5sc
(Ch5, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 2sc), 3sc
(Ch8, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 5sc), 3sc, fasten off with a long yarn tail

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Using the Top of the Head as a guide, cut a piece of plastic canvas slightly smaller than the Top of the Head. It should reach the midway point of Rnd 9.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Cut 5 more plastic canvas squares the same size as the one just measured to make the Body Structure and set aside for later.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: With a long piece of yarn in {@b A} to match the Top of the Head, attach the plastic canvas squares to the bottom side of the Top of the Head square.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: With a long piece of yarn in {@b B} to match the Body, sew the 5 plastic canvas squares together in a cube shape. Leave the top side of the cube open and stuff with polyester fiberfill. Set the Body Structure aside and continue to the Body crochet section.

items: Flip the Top of the Head over so the bottom side with the plastic canvas is face up. Insert the crochet hook into the back bump of one of the hdc stitches of Row 9 on the Head and attach {@b B}.
{@i Note: The crochet hook should be inserted going toward the middle of the Head.}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Working in the back bump of each hdc stitch of Rnd 9, [7sc, dec] × 8. (64)

items: Insert the plastic canvas cube into the Body—be sure to stuff the cube with polyfill before inserting. Add or remove rows, as needed, if the sides of the Body do not reach the end of the plastic canvas cube.

items.name: Rnd 21:
entries: Slst, and ch1 at the end of Rnd 20. Starting in the same stitch as the slst [6sc blo, dec] × 8. (56)

items: Fasten off and use the yarn tail to close the remaining 8 stitches. Weave in ends.
Fold down Rnd 10 of the Top of the Head across the top of the Body and sew into place. Fasten off and weave in any ends.
{@b (MAKE 2)}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: With {@b A}, ch7, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 6sc. Fasten off with a long yarn tail. (6)

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b D}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: 4sc in ring. Change to {@b A} and slst, ch1 at the start of the row. (4)

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: 4inc blo. Change to {@b E} and slst, ch1 at the start of the row. (8)

items.name: Rnd 3:
entries: Blo [inc, sc] × 4. Slst and fasten off with a long yarn tail. (12)

items: With {@b E}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 11:
entries: 2sc, dec, 2sc, 5inc flo, 2sc. Fasten off with a long yarn tail and stuff with polyester fiberfill. (17)

items: Flatten the bottom of the Skull and sew closed with yarn tail. With {@b D}, sew X-shaped eyes over Rnd 7 with 3 stitches between and an upside down V-shaped nose on Rnd 9, centered between the eyes.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b G}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off with a long tail.
With {@b C}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail.

items.name: Assemble the top half of the Arrow:
entries: Insert the closed side of Arrow Shaft into the bottom of the Arrowhead. Flatten the Arrowhead and sew closed.

items: With {@b E}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch13, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook—inc, 3sc, 2dec, 3sc, inc, ch1 and turn (12)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail.

items.name: Assemble the bottom half of the Arrow:
entries: Lay the closed side of the Arrow Shaft over the top of the Arrow Fletching. Using the tail end from the Arrow Fletching, sew both pieces together.

items: With {@b F}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 4:
entries: [Sc, dec] × 3, change to {@b G} at the end of the row (6)

items.name: Rnds 5–9:
entries: 6sc, change to {@b F} at the end of Rnd 9 (6)

items.name: Rnd 12:
entries: 4sc, inc, sc, inc, 6sc, inc, sc, inc, 2sc (22)

items: Fasten off with a long yarn tail.
With {@b C}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off with a long yarn tail.

items.name: Assemble the Sword:
entries: Insert the closed side of the Blade into the Sword Hilt and fold the gold piece flat across the Blade. Sew the Hilt closed using the yarn tail from the Hilt.

items.name: Eyes and Eyebrows:
entries: Pick a side of the Cube as the front. Attach the Eyes between Rnds 10 and 15. Attach the Eyebrows above the Eyes, just covering the top inner corners of the Eyes and angled downward in an angry expression.

items.name: Skull:
entries: Attach to the top left corner of the Cube's Head.

items.name: Top Half of Arrow:
entries: Attach to the right side of the Cube with the Arrow pointing downward.

items.name: Bottom Half of Arrow:
entries: Attach to the left side of the Cube with the Fletching pointing upward. Line up the Arrow top and bottom to appear as if it were going through the Cube.

items.name: Sword:
entries: Attach to the top right corner of the Cube's Head.

items.name: Mouth:
entries: Using a 12 in. / 30.5 cm length of {@b D} and a tapestry needle, stitch a mouth just below the Eyes between Rnds 17 and 18 in a )—( shape.

crochetPattern.name: Gloves of Missile Snaring
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Caron {@i Simply Soft} (100% acrylic, 315 yd. / 288 m per 6 oz. / 170 g per skein)
DK weight (#3 light) yarn, shown in Lion Brand {@i Truboo} (100% rayon, 241 yd. / 220 m per 3½ oz. / 100 g skein)
gauge: 6 dc and 10 dc rows = 4 in. / 10 cm
Make sure to check your gauge.

items.name: Surface Slip Stitch (surface slst):
entries: Like a regular slip stitch, but instead of working into the edge, insert your hook into the front surface of the fabric to create a visible line.

notes: This pattern is worked using the tapestry technique. With tapestry technique, you change colors as indicated, carrying the unused color inside the stitches throughout the row. This pattern can be worked by following the chart or using the written instructions.

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: (Plum) Ch31, working back, sc into 2nd ch from hook (counts as first sc). (Plum) 6sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 14sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 7sc. (30)

items.name: Row 4:
entries: (Plum) 5sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 10sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 5sc (30)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: (Plum) 4sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 8sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 4sc (30)

items.name: Row 7:
entries: (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 4sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc (30)

items.name: Row 10:
entries: (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 4sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc (30)

items.name: Row 11:
entries: (Silver) 2sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) 4sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) 2sc (30)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 6sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc (30)

items.name: Row 14:
entries: (Silver) 2sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) 4sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) 2sc (30)

items.name: Row 19:
entries: (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 16sc (30)

items.name: Row 21:
entries: (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 16sc (30)

items.name: Row 22:
entries: (Plum) 15sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) 3sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc (30)

items.name: Row 24:
entries: (Plum) 15sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) 3sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 3sc, (Silver) sc (30)

items.name: Row 25:
entries: (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) 3sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 16sc (30)

items.name: Row 27:
entries: (Plum) sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 2sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) 16sc (30)

items.name: Row 30:
entries: (Plum) 15sc, (Silver) 2sc, (Plum) 11sc, (Silver) sc, (Plum) sc. (30) Fasten off and weave in loose ends.

items: With 2 strands of Silver, surface slst along the pixel pattern of the Glove Panels, highlighting the web patterning, making the 2 Panels mirror images, and following the designs below.

Fold Glove Panel in half lengthwise with surface stitching facing the inside. With {@b A}, join the side seam together. Starting at top corner, crochet together along the inside side edge, stopping after 10sc. Fasten off. Starting at the bottom corner, crochet together along the inside side edge, stopping after 10sc. This leaves a 10sc gap in the center of the seam, creating a thumbhole. Repeat with second Glove Panel. Fasten off and weave in any ends. Turn both Gloves right side out.

crochetPattern.name: Mantle of Spell Resistance
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)
notions: Gray felt or craft foam and glue (optional)
gauge: 12 dc and 8 dc rows = 4 in. / 10 cm
Make sure to check your gauge. If your gauge is significantly different than this, you may need to adjust your hook size.

items.name: Picot:
entries: Ch3, slst in 2nd ch from hook

items: With largest hook and {@b MC}, work from the top (collar) down.

items.name: Row 9:
entries: Ch2, turn, dcinc, 16dc, dcinc, 12dc, dcinc, 26dc, dcinc, 12dc, dcinc, 16dc, dcinc (94)

items.name: Row 11:
entries: Ch2, turn, dcinc, ch4, sk4, [4dc, dcinc] × 19 (120)

items: Place a stitch marker at the end of this row.
In the next row, when you get to the ch4, work into the top loop of each ch.

items.name: Row 24:
entries: Ch2, turn, dc in first st, 238fpdc across until last st, dc in last st (240)

items.name: Row 25:
entries: Ch2, turn, dcinc in first st, [13dc, dc3tog,13dc, 3dc in next st] × 7, 13dc, dc3tog, 11dc, dcinc × 2 (241)

items.name: Rows 26–48:
entries: Ch2, turn, dcinc in first st, [13dc, dc3tog, 13dc, 3dc in next st] × 7, 13dc, dc3tog, 13dc, dcinc (241)

items.name: Row 49:
entries: Ch5, turn, slst in 3rd ch from hook, (tr, dc in same st), [13dc, dc3tog, 13dc, (dc, tr, picot, tr, dc in same st)] × 7, 13dc, dc3tog, 13dc, (dc, tr in same st), picot. Do NOT fasten off your work, continue on as follows.

items.name: Border:
entries: 96sc evenly up the side of the Cloak. [Sc, dc, sc] in corner st at collar, 82sc across collar, [sc, dc, sc] in next corner of collar, 96sc evenly down the side of the cloak. Slst and fasten off. Weave in all the ends.

items: With largest hook and {@b A}
{@i Note: The right side of the cloak is the side with the fpdc of Row 24 facing out (kind of looks like a reverse seam).}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: With the right side facing you, attach yarn to the picot at the bottom corner, ch1, sc in same st, 97sc up the side of the Cloak, (3sc) in corner, 84sc, (3sc) in corner, 98sc down the side of the Cloak (283)

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, (working up the side of the Cloak again) (2sc) in the same stitch, 98sc, (3sc) in corner, 86sc, (3sc) in corner, 98sc, (2sc) in last st (292)

items.name: Row 3:
entries: Ch2, turn, (dc, sc in same st), 100sc, (dc, picot, dc in corner), 88sc, (dc, picot, dc in corner), 100sc, (sc, dc in same st) (298)

items: Fasten off and weave in ends.
{@b (MAKE 4)}
With largest hook and {@b A}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, sc in 2nd ch from hook and across (3)

items.name: Row 10:
entries: Ch1, do NOT turn, work 2sc, dec evenly down the side edge. You are not working all the way down. (3)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
Pin these Cloak Details onto the Cloak at the edges, 2 on each side, evenly. Sew into place.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With medium hook and {@b A}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, sc in 2nd ch from hook and across (3)

items.name: Row 9:
entries: Ch1, do NOT turn, work 3sc evenly down the side edge. You are not working all the way down. (3)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
Decide whether you want your collar standing up or lying down. Then pin the Collar Details in place, on the side that will face out, in the corner point of the collar. Sew in place.

instructions.name: Clasp
entries: {@i Note: The clasp is made up of 3 pieces.}

entries.name: Piece 1

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch5, 2sc in 2nd ch from hook, 2sc, 4sc in last ch. Continue working on the opposite side of the foundation chain: 2sc, 2sc in last ch, slst to first st to join. (12)

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: Ch1, inc, 4sc, inc2, 4sc, inc, slst to first st (16)

items.name: Rnd 3:
entries: Ch1, inc, 6sc, inc2, 6sc, inc, slst to first st (20)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail (about 18 in. / 46 cm) for sewing. Embroider the symbol on front of Piece 1 with embroidery thread and tapestry needle. (See Clasp close-up for symbol design)

entries.name: Piece 2
items: Make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: 6sc in ring, slst to first st (6)

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: Ch1, [2inc, sc] × 2, slst to first st (10)

items.name: Rnd 3:
entries: Ch1, sc, 2inc, 3sc, 2inc, 2sc, slst to first st (14)

items.name: Rnd 5:
entries: Ch1, 2sc, inc, 2sc, inc, 5sc, inc, 2sc, inc, 3sc, slst to first st (22)

items.name: Rnd 6:
entries: Ch1, 4sc, inc, picot, inc, [4sc, inc] × 2, picot, inc, 4sc, inc, slst to first st (30)

items: Fasten off and weave in ends.

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Turn, picot, slst2, picot, sc in same, 3sc, inc, picot, inc, 4sc, picot, slst in same (16) Fasten off and weave in ends.

entries.name: Assembling Clasp
items: Layer Piece 1 on top of Piece 2 and sew into place. Do not cut the yarn yet. Now layer Pieces 1 and 2 on top of Piece 3 so the center picot of Piece 3 lines up with the center bottom picot of Piece 2. Pin the picots together and spread the Piece 3 wings so that they come just to the outside edge of Piece 2. Pin in place. Now, using the rest of the length of yarn, sew the wings into place.
{@i Note: If you would like to cover the back of your Clasp, you can cut a piece of felt or craft foam and glue it into place.}
In the area where you put the stitch marker in Row 11 of the Cloak, sew the Clasp using just the center back of the Clasp like it's a button.

crochetPattern.name: Mimic Dice Bag
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Lion Brand {@i Heartland Yarn} (100% acrylic, 251 yd. / 230 m per 5 oz. / 142 g skein)
Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
notions: 2 craft magnets, ½ in. / 1 cm or smaller in diameter
Plastic canvas circle, 4 in. / 10 cm in diameter
Pipe cleaner or craft wire, approximately 6 in. to 8 in. / 15–20 cm long
gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Keep your tension the same throughout the project to ensure panels line up correctly.
notes: The box is worked in panels with different interior and exterior colors. The panels are joined together to make the box. The strips are worked separately and attached afterward.

items: {@b With A}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch25, starting in 2nd ch from the hook and working in the back bumps of the chain stitches, 24hdc, ch2 and turn (24)

items.name: Rows 4–9:
entries: Repeat Rows 2 and 3. Fasten off with a short tail at the end of Row 9. (24)

items: {@b With C}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch25, starting in 2nd ch from the hook and working in the back bumps of the chain stitches, 24hdc, ch2 and turn (24)

items.name: Rows 2–9:
entries: 24hdc, ch2 and turn. Fasten off with a short tail at the end of Row 9. (24)

items: Using one of the Base Panels as a guide for size, precut a piece of plastic canvas to fit between the 2 Base Panels. Size needed is approximately 5½ in. x 3 in. / 14 cm x 8 cm. Set aside for now.

items.name: To start, join the Box Base Panels:
entries: Place the Base Panels together with the right sides facing outward. Attach {@b B} to the top of one of the short sides of the Base Panels (the Exterior Panel should be facing you).

items: {@i Note: There are no defined spaces to crochet into on the short sides, so space out the hdc stitches as evenly as possible.}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: 10hdc evenly down the short side. Continuing around to the long side, hdc inc in the first stitch of the long row, 22hdc across, and hdc inc in the last stitch of the long row. 10hdc evenly up the next short side. Before continuing on to the last side, insert the precut piece of plastic canvas between the Box Base Exterior and Interior Panels. Then, hdc inc in the first stitch of the final long side, 22hdc across, and hdc inc in the final stitch. Slst and ch1 into the first stitch of the row. (72)

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Working in the 3rd loop of the hdc stitches in Row 1, slst in each stitch around (72)

items: {@i Note: In Row 3, you will make the corner chain strips.}

items.name: Row 3:
entries: Working around the slsts of the previous row and into the 3rd loop space of Row 1, 11sc, [make corner chain strip—ch14, starting in 3rd back bump space from the hook, 12hdc back down the chain], starting in the next stitch on Row 3, 14sc, [make corner chain strip—ch14, starting in 3rd back bump space from the hook, 12hdc back down the chain], starting in the next stitch on Row 3, 12sc, [make corner chain strip—ch14, starting in 3rd back bump space from the hook, 12hdc back down the chain], starting in the next stitch on Row 3, 14sc, [make corner chain strip—ch14, starting in 3rd back bump space from the hook, 12hdc back down the chain], sc in the last stitch of the row. (72)

items: {@i Note: Leave the 4 corner chain strips loose for now and continue to work the Side Panels.}
Change to {@b A} and slst, ch2 at the end of the row.

items.name: Row 4:
entries: 72hdc blo, skipping over the 4 strips made in Row 3. The strips can be pulled to the front of the stitches and left loose for now. Slst in the first stitch of the row, ch2, and turn. (72)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: 72hdc flo. Slst in the first stitch of the row, ch2, and turn. (72 hdc)

items: Change to {@b B} at the end of Row 10, slst and ch2, do not turn.

items.name: Row 11:
entries: 72hdc. Slst and ch1 at the end of the row. Place a stitch marker in the 48th stitch of the row and in the last stitch of the row for later. (72)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Working in the 3rd loop of the previous row's hdc stitches, 72sc. Place a stitch marker in the 13th stitch of the row. (72)

items.name: To make the inside of the box:
entries: Change to {@b C} and slst, ch2 at the end of the row.

items.name: Rows 14–20:
entries: 72hdc. At the end of Row 20, flip the section made with {@b C} inside the piece and make sure the final row reaches the bottom. Add or remove rows if needed and fasten off with a long tail. (72)

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Cut out a piece of plastic canvas using the long and short sides of the Box Panels as measurements. Size needed for the short sides is approximately 3½ in. x 2¾ in. / 9 cm x 7 cm; approximately 6½ in. x 2¾ in. / 16.5 cm x 7 cm for the long sides.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Using a 12 in. / 30.5 cm length of yarn in {@b A} for each side of the plastic canvas (you'll need 4 pieces of yarn), sew the cut-out pieces of the plastic canvas together in a rectangular shape with no bottom.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Insert the plastic canvas rectangle inside the Box Body of the chest and flip the panel created with {@b C} inside.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Using the long length of yarn left at the end of Box Body Row 20, sew evenly around the bottom, attaching Box Body Row 20 to Box Base Exterior Panel Row 9. It is easier to complete this step if you flip the side you are attaching outward.

items.name: Step 5:
entries: Pin the corner chain strips made in Row 3 of the Box Body Panels in place at the 4 corners of the rectangle. The tops of the strips should reach Row 11 of the Box Body. Sew them in place with a length of yarn in {@b B} through the back loops only. Fasten off and weave in any ends.

items: With {@b B}, and with the back of the Box Body facing you, attach yarn to Row 11, stitch 48 on the Body, previously marked with a stitch marker. This stitch should be located in one of the corners of the rectangle. Adjust placement accordingly if they do not line up with the corners.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: 24hdc flo. The last hdc should be in the stitch marker placed in the last hdc of Row 11 on the Box Body. Change to {@b A}, ch2 and turn. (24)

items: Change to {@b B} at the end of Row 18, ch2 and turn.

items.name: Row 20:
entries: Working in the 3rd loop of the previous row's hdc stitches, 24hdc (24)

items: Change to {@b C} at the end of Row 20, ch2 and turn.
Change to {@b B} at the end of Row 39, ch2 and turn.

items.name: Row 40:
entries: 24hdc. Fasten off with a long tail for sewing. (24)

items: {@b (MAKE 4, 2 EXTERIOR AND 2 INTERIOR)}

items.name: Exterior Sides (make 2):
entries: With {@b A}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Interior Sides (make 2):
entries: With {@b C}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Row 4:
entries: Inc, sc, inc, 6sc, inc, sc, inc, ch1 and turn (16)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: Inc, [4sc, inc] x 3, ch1 and turn (20)

items.name: On all 4 Sides:
entries: Change to {@b B} at the end of Row 6, ch2 and turn.

items.name: Row 7:
entries: 23hdc, hdc inc in the last stitch of the row. Working across the bottom edge, 11hdc. Hdc in the starting space of Row 6. Slst to the first stitch made in Row 7 and fasten off. (37)

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Cut out a piece of plastic canvas using the section made with C on the Box Lid as a guide. Size needed is approximately 6¾ x 6 in. / 17 cm x 15 cm.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Using a tapestry needle and {@b B}, attach Row 40 of the Box Lid to the open back loops left on Row 11 of the Box Body. Insert the plastic canvas piece between the two sides of the Box Lid before continuing to Step 3.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Close up one side of the Lid. With the back of the Box facing you, attach {@b B} to the bottom right side of the Lid in the same space as the first stitch of Row 1. Working up the side of the Lid and crocheting through the Interior and Exterior Sides at the same time, 26hdc. The last hdc should be in the same space as the first stitch of Row 19. Slst to the first stitch of Row 19 and fasten off.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Close up the other side of the Lid. With the front of the Box facing you, attach {@b B} to the bottom right side of the Lid in the same space as the last stitch of Row 19. Working up the side of the Lid and crocheting through both Interior and Exterior Sides at the same time, 26hdc. The last hdc should be in the same space as the last stitch of Row 1. Slst to the last stitch of Row 1 and fasten off.

items.name: Step 5:
entries: Cut a plastic canvas circle in half. Trim both halves to size by using the Box Lid Side as reference. Size needed is approximately 3 in. x 1¾ in. / 8 cm x 4.5 cm.

items.name: Step 6:
entries: With a tapestry needle and {@b B}, place the Exterior and Interior Panels of the Box Lid Sides together with the right sides facing outward and sew together through the back loops. Insert the half circle plastic canvas between the 2 pieces before sewing the bottom closed.

items.name: Step 7:
entries: With a tapestry needle and {@b B}, sew each Lid Side to either side of the Lid Top with A facing outward. Wrap the sides of the Lid around the curve of the Lid Sides and pin in place to ensure that they are sewn on evenly.

items: With {@b D}, and with the Body facing you, attach yarn to the open front loops of Row 12, stitch 13 on the Body.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: [3sc, (ch4, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, slst, 2sc)] x 7, 3sc. Fasten off with a short tail and weave in any ends.

items: With {@b D}, and with the top of the Lid facing you, attach yarn to the open front loops on Row 21 of the Box Lid.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Sc, (ch5, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, slst, sc, 2hdc), [starting in the next space, 3sc, (ch4, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, slst, 2sc)] x 6, 3sc, (ch5, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, slst, sc, 2hdc), sc. Fasten off with a short tail and weave in any ends.

items: With {@b F}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off with a long tail.

Insert craft wire or pipe cleaner into Tongue and stuff the bottom half of the Tongue lightly with polyester fiberfill to give the base some shape. Pin in place on the bottom of the Box toward the back and centered. Sew in place.
With {@b E}, make a magic ring.
Change to {@b D}.

items.name: Rnd 4:
entries: Working through the blo of the slsts in Rnd 3 and through the blo spaces previously worked in Rnd 2, [sc, inc] x 6 (18)

items: Change to {@b B}.

items.name: Rnd 6:
entries: Slst and ch1 at the start of the row. Then, starting in the flo of the same stitch as the slst, 7sc, 2inc, 7sc, 2inc. Slst and ch1. (22)

items.name: Rnd 7:
entries: 8sc, 3inc, 8sc, 3inc. Slst and fasten off with a long tail. (28)

items.name: Step 1:
entries: With {@b G} and a tapestry needle, sew a line vertically down the middle of the Eye across Rnd 1—this is the pupil. Make 3 passes with the yarn to make the pupil wider.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: With {@b D} and a tapestry needle, sew a small line horizontally across a small part of the pupil and into the iris of the Eye. The end result should look like a cat's eye with a shine mark.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Pin the Eye in place, centered slightly above Row 18 of the Box Lid. Using a tapestry needle and the yarn tail, sew in place. Sew between Rnds 6 and 7 of the Eye around the top and bottom to make it bulge so it looks more menacing.

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b B}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch91, starting in 2nd ch from the hook and in the back bumps of the chain spaces, 89hdc back down. In the last stitch, 6hdc in the same space and slst in the bottom of the 89th stitch. Fasten off with a long tail. (95)

items.name: Step 1:
entries: With craft glue or hot glue, attach the magnets to the Box Chain Strips. Attach 1 magnet to the underside of the 6hdc made at the end of the Strip and attach 1 magnet between stitches 5 and 8 of each Strip (or approximately 1½ in. / 38 mm from the top of the strip). Make sure the magnets are facing the correct way to link together before gluing in place. Let the glue dry fully before moving to Step 2.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Pin the Strips in place on either side of the Eye and around the full width of the Box. They should be approximately 1 in. / 2.5 cm from the sides of the chest. The 6hdc part of the Strip should hang approximately 1½ in. / 38 mm off the front of the chest.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Using a tapestry needle and the yarn tail of the Strip, sew in place on the chest. Make sure the Strips line up evenly and the magnets can reach each other before sewing the Strips into place.

crochetPattern.name: Mind Flayer
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)

items.name: Color D (belt, chest, and shoulder armor):

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.

items.name: Surface Slip Stitch (surface slst):
entries: Like a regular slip stitch, but instead of working into the edge, insert your hook into the front surface of the fabric to create a visible line.

notes: The project is worked primarily in single crochet stitches in the amigurumi style. The Cape is worked separately and attached.
Stitches in parentheses are worked into the same space.

items: With {@b B}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 13:
entries: This round creates the Tentacles—four total. 6sc, [sc flo, ch 12, starting in 2nd ch from hook: [inc, sc] × 4, 3sc] × 4, sc flo, 3sc. (24)

items.name: Rnd 14:
entries: 24sc. The sc stitches behind the Tentacles go in the unworked back loops of the previous round. (24)

items: Insert 12-mm safety eyes between Rnds 11 and 12 with 5 spaces between. Stuff the Head medium firm with polyester fiberfill.
Fasten off and close up the last 8 stitches with the yarn tail. Weave in ends.
With {@b B}, embroider eyebrows just above the eyes between Rnds 9 and 11. Angle the eyebrows downward for a scowling face.

items.name: Optional:
entries: To shape the eyes, use the yarn tail to sew back and forth around the outer sides of the eyes and pull tight until the eyes are sunken into a preferred depth.

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b B}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: Sc, [sc (ch5, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook 2slst, 2sc)] × 4, sc (6)

items: Change to {@b D}.

items.name: Rnd 7:
entries: Sc in each stitch by working through the blo of the slst in the previous round and through the sc stitch of Rnd 5 (9)

items: Change to {@b A}.

items.name: Rnd 9:
entries: Sc in each stitch by working through the blo of the slst in the previous round and through the sc stitch of Rnd 7 (9)

items: Stuff the Arm medium firm with polyester fiberfill.

items.name: Rnd 15:
entries: Fold in half and 4sc across the folded stitches, working through both sides (4)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail to attach to the Body Base.
With {@b A}, ch12.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Join the ch stitches into a loop by making a sc in the first ch stitch of the round. Sc in remaining ch spaces. (12)

items: Stuff the Body Base medium firm with polyester fiberfill.
Fasten off and close up the last 6 stitches with yarn tail. Weave in ends.

Attach {@b A} to the last stitch of Rnd 12 on the Body Base. The first row is worked in the open front loop spaces. The Body Base should be upside down and your crochet hook should be inserted with the tip pointed toward the bottom of the Body Base.

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Working into the sc stitches of Row 11 and the open front loop spaces of Rnd 23 on the Body Base, [4sc, in the 5th stitch space insert hook into the same front loop space as the last stitch and into the next stitch over on Row 11] × 6, ch1 and turn. There should be 6 remaining front loop spaces that are unworked. (30)

items.name: Row 13:
entries: [4sc, inc] × 6, fasten off with a short yarn tail (36)

items.name: Row 14:
entries: Attach {@b D} to the starting sc of Row 13, [inc, 5sc] × 6, ch1 and do not turn (42)

items.name: Row 15:
entries: Working up the side of the Body Robe, sc approximately 13 stitches. 2sc in the front loop spaces on the Body Base (you will work these stitches next to the sc stitches from Row 1 that are already there). Working down the other side of the Body Robe, sc approximately 13 stitches. Slst at the starting sc of Row 14, fasten off, and weave in ends. (28)

items: With {@b D},

Insert crochet hook between Rows 12 and 13 of the Body Robe and surface slst around the Body. The surface slst spaces above the center of the Robe opening are worked through the {@b D} sc spaces at the top of the opening. Fasten off and weave in any ends.
With {@b D}, ch3.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, 2sc, ch1 and turn (2)

items.name: Row 3:
entries: Starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, 3sc, ch1 and turn (3)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: Starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, 4sc, ch1 and turn (4)

items.name: Row 6:
entries: 3sc, ch1 and turn—do not work a stitch in the last space (3)

items.name: Row 8:
entries: 2sc, ch1 and turn—do not work a stitch in the last space (2)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail.
With {@b C}, ch7.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Starting in 2nd ch from the hook 6sc, ch1 and turn (6)

items: Fasten off, leaving a short tail.
With {@b C}, attach yarn to the starting chain row of the Cape Top with a slst.

items.name: Row 26:
entries: Sc approximately 32 stitches up the side of the Cape. Inc, 16sc, inc across the top of the Cape. Working down the other side of the Cape, sc approximately 32 stitches, slst into the starting sc from Row 25. Fasten off and weave in any ends.

items.name: Arms:
entries: Attach to the Body between Rnds 3 and 6, angled forward.

items.name: Armor:
entries: Pin the Chest and Shoulder Armor in place around the Chest and Arms. The middle point should sit between Rnds 3 and 6 of the Body and the side points should sit on top of the Arms. The start and end 2sc rows should be connected together to form the piece into one loop.

items.name: Head:
entries: Attach the Head to the top of the Body at the opening. The Head is slightly angled and attached between Rnds 2 and 4 of the Head.

items.name: Cape:
entries: Center the Cape Top on the back of the Head and attach between Rows 2 and 4 of the Cape Top and Rnds 11 and 14 of the Head. Attach the section of the Cape where the Cape Top and Bottom are connected to the base of the neck (where it connects to the Head).

crochetPattern.name: Minsc's Hat Of Winter Warming
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Lion Brand {@i Made with Love the Cottony One} (60% cotton, 40% acrylic, 185 yd. / 169 m per 3½ oz. / 100 g skein)
gauge: 16 dc and 10 dc rows = 4 in. / 10 cm
notes: This pattern is worked in the intarsia style. In intarsia, you do NOT work over the nonworking color; each color section is attached to its own yarn bobbin or ball of yarn.
This pattern can be worked by following the chart or using the written instructions.

Every row is worked in sc blo, to create the ridging effect. In each row, skip the final st, and work the last sc into the ch1 of the previous row to create a flat edge. The pattern is worked as a flat panel, and then joined into a tube and closed up to create a hat.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: (White) Ch51, working back, sc into 2nd ch from hook (counts as first sc) (50)

items: Fold the Panel widthwise, and slst Row 73 to the starting chain of Row 1, using front loops only. Ch2, and cut a long tail before fastening off. This turns the Panel into a tube.

With a tapestry needle, run White through the tops of the stitches of the open side closest to the circular pattern and pull tight to close up the top of the hat. If needed, sew the final hole closed after cinching tightly. Weave in any ends.
Fold the open bottom of the hat upward to form the final brim.

crochetPattern.name: Owlbear Cub
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 347 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)

items.name: Color B (eye mask, cheek pieces, and paw pads):

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.

items.name: DcXtog in the same stitch / Bobble Stitch:
entries: This is often called a "bobble stitch," but this pattern uses "dcXtog in the same stitch" because it allows you to vary the number of dc5tog to make smaller or larger bobbles, as needed.
For example, to dc3tog in the same stitch,
(1) yarn over, insert hook into stitch, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through 2 loops (leaving 2 on hook);
(2) yarn over, insert hook into same stitch, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through 2 loops (leaving 3 on hook);
(3) yarn over, insert hook into same stitch, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through 2 loops, yarn over and pull through all 4 loops on hook to finish.

items.name: Invisible Decrease (inv dec):
entries: Insert hook into front loop of indicated stitch, insert hook into front loop of next stitch (3 loops on hook). Yarn over, pull through 2 loops. Yarn over, pull through remaining 2 loops on hook.

notes: This pattern works well in other weights of yarn (for example, chenille and blanket yarns) and an appropriately sized hook. Using heavier-weight yarn, the resulting Owlbear Cub will be much larger and more cuddly.

items: With {@b A}, make a magic ring.
Color changing begins here. Use {@b B} for stitches in {@i italics}.

items.name: Rnd 34:
entries: 26sc, dec, {@i [2sc, dec] × 2, sc,} sc, {@i dec, [2sc, dec] × 2} (42)

items: Continue in {@b A}.
Insert the safety eyes between Rnds 36 and 37, approximately 8 stitch spaces apart (each eye is positioned roughly 4 stitch spaces from the center of the face).
Fasten off, leaving a short tail to close the piece and weave in the ends.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b B}, ch19.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in 2nd chain from the hook, 3slst, 3sc, 6hdc, 3sc, 3slst (18)

items: Fasten off, leaving a medium tail for sewing in place around each eye.
With {@b C}, ch5.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook, slst, sc, 2hdc, ch1 and turn (4)

items: Fasten off, leaving a medium tail for sewing to the head.
With {@b A}, ch2.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook, sc, ch1 and turn (1)

items.name: Rnd 13:
entries: Inc, 14sc, inc. Do NOT ch1 and turn. (18)

items.name: Edge 1a:
entries: Rotate the piece so you are working down the side of the triangle toward the starting point. Make approximately 11hdc, then sc, slst to the starting point. Ch1 and turn. (13)

items: Fasten off, leaving a short tail.

items.name: Edge 2a:
entries: Reattach your yarn to the opposite corner of your piece from where you started the first edge. Working down the side toward the starting point, make approximately 11hdc, then sc, slst to the starting point. Ch1 and turn. (13)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing to the body.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b A}, ch6.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from your hook, inc, 3sc, 2inc in the end of the chain, working down the unworked side of the starting chain, 3sc, inc (14)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long yarn tail for sewing (you will close the hole as you sew).
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b A}, ch5.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from your hook, inc, 2sc, working around the end of the chain, 2inc, working down the unused (back) side of the chain, 2sc, inc (12)

items: Continue working in a spiral.

The inc stitch should be located at the center-back of the piece, and the dec stitch should be located at the center-front. If differences in tension, yarn weight, or hook size have shifted your stitches, adjust the position of the inc/dec stitches as needed (you can check the center of your piece by flattening it in line with the center toe).
In Rnds 15 and 16, the dec stitch should be located at the center-back of the piece and the inc stitch should be located at the center-front.
Fasten off, leaving a long yarn tail for sewing (you will close the hole as you sew).

instructions.name: Rear Paw Pads

entries.name: Top Pad
items: With {@b B}, ch6.

items.name: Rnd 1:

items: Fasten off leaving a long tail for sewing and for stitching claws on each toe.

entries.name: Bottom Pad
items: With {@b B}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a medium tail for sewing.

instructions.name: Front Paw Pads
entries: {@b (MAKE 2 EACH OF TOP AND BOTTOM PADS)}

entries.name: Top Pad
items: With {@b B}, ch4.

entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from your hook, inc, sc, inc (5)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing and for stitching claws on each toe.
