entries: 6sc into ring, slst into the first stitch to secure it (6)

items: Fasten off, leaving a medium tail for sewing.
With {@b A}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a medium tail for sewing.

entries.name: Upper
items: With {@b A}, ch4.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from your hook, slst, 2sc, ch1 and turn (3)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.

entries.name: Lower
items: With {@b A}, ch4.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd chain from your hook, slst, 2sc, ch1 and turn (3)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Pin the Cheek Pieces around the eyes, along the edge of the color change, to create the Owlbear's eye mask. The inner tips of the Cheek Pieces should come together at the center of the face and will be covered by the Beak. Once you are happy with their position, use the yarn tails to sew them in place and weave in your ends.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: After the Cheek Pieces are sewn on, position the Headpiece with the point just above where the two Cheek Pieces meet in the center of the face. Each side of the Headpiece extends diagonally over each eye to make a V-shape. Edge 1 and Edge 2 of the Headpiece should be folded inward near the center point so that they stand up toward the back of the Headpiece, creating head feathers. Sew these folded edges in place. Sew the rest of the Headpiece in place and weave in ends.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: After attaching the Headpiece, you can attach the Beak. Fold the Beak in half lengthwise and position it so that it covers the bottom point of the Headpiece and the meeting point of the two Cheek Pieces. Sew the Beak in place and weave in ends.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Pin the pieces in place securely to make sure they are correctly positioned before you sew them on. Start by pinning both Rear Legs in place at the base of the Body, roughly halfway between the front and back sides. The Rear Legs should splay out to the sides. Next, pin the two Front Legs in place so that they sit in front of and between the Rear Legs, with the bottom of the front paws touching the floor and nearly touching each other (angled slightly inward). The top of the Front Legs should be positioned just below the decrease for the neck. Once you are happy with the position of the limbs, sew them in place.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: The Paw Pads should be pinned in place on the bottom of the Front and Rear Legs. After sewing the Top Pad to each foot, use the remaining yarn tail to sew a long stitch on the top of each toe to make claws.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Sew the Tail in place near the bottom curve of the Body, centered between the Back Legs.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Pin the Chest Feathers in place. They should sit like a necklace around the base of the Owlbear Cub's neck, with the Upper Feather piece on top and the Lower piece underneath (to fit between the Front Legs). When you are happy with their position, use {@b A} to sew the top edge of each piece in place so that the Chest Feathers look connected to the Body.

crochetPattern.name: Platinum Dragon Cowl
yarn: DK weight (#3 light) yarn, shown in Lion Brand {@i Truboo} (100% rayon, 241 yd. / 220 m per 3½ oz. / 100 g skein)
gauge: 16 dc and 10 dc rows = 4 in. / 10 cm
Make sure to check your gauge.

items.name: Crab Stitch / Reverse Single Crochet:
entries: Ch1. Without turning your work, insert the hook from front to back in the first single crochet stitch. Yarn over your hook and draw up a loop. Yarn over and draw it through both loops.

items.name: Surface Slip Stitch (surface slst):
entries: Like a regular slip stitch, but instead of working into the edge, insert your hook into the front surface of the fabric to create a visible line.

items: Work together 2 strands of yarn throughout.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch80, slst together into a round. Ch1, sc in first sc (counts as first sc). [Ch1, sk st, sc in next st] × 38. Ch1, slst to close. (80)

items.name: Rnds 2–15:
entries: Slst to first ch sp. Ch1, sc in same ch sp. [Ch1, sc in next ch sp] × 39. Ch1, slst to close. (80)

items.name: Rnd 16:
entries: Slst to first ch sp. Ch1, sc in same ch sp. [Ch1, insert hook through scale, sc in next ch sp] × 39. Ch1, slst to chose. Fasten off. (80 sts / 40 scales)

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Returning to Rnd 15, surface slst through each ch sp, inserting hook through a scale, ch1, and surface slst through the next ch sp. Repeat around, attaching 40 scales per round.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Repeat for Rnds 12–14, working each layer of scales above the previous layer.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: To finish, work a border in crab stitch along the starting chain. Slst to close, fasten off.

crochetPattern.name: Purple Worm Scarf
yarn: Bulky weight (#5 bulky) yarn, shown in Bernat {@i Velvet} (100% polyester, 315 yd. / 288 m per 10½ oz. / 300 g skein)
gauge: 12 dc and 8 rows = 4 in / 10 cm

items.name: Backpost Half Double Crochet (bphdc):
entries: Yarn over and insert the hook from the back to front to back around the post of the stitch of the previous row, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through all 3 loops on your hook

items.name: Picot Stitch (picot):
entries: Ch2, slst into the front loop of last st made

items.name: Dc5tog in the same stitch / Bobble Stitch:
entries: Double crochet 5 stitches together in the same stitch to create a bobble

items.name: Spike Stitch (spike st):
entries: The spike stitch is worked using the same steps as a single crochet stitch, but instead of working into the current row, drop down and work it into the row below the current row.

crochetPattern.name: Red Dragon
crochetPattern.sizeNote: Size will vary depending on yarn weight, hook size, and tension.
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)
notions: 18-gauge floral wire, 8 to 10 pieces of varying length as specified in the instructions
gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.

items.name: DcXtog in the same stitch / Bobble Stitch:
entries: This is often called a "bobble stitch," but this pattern uses "dcXtog in the same stitch" because it allows you to vary the number of dc5tog to make smaller or larger bobbles as needed.
For example, to dc3tog in the same stitch:
1) Yarn over, insert hook into stitch, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through 2 loops (leaving 2 on hook).
2) Yarn over, insert hook into the same stitch, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through 2 loops (leaving 3 on hook).
3) Yarn over, insert hook into the same stitch, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through 2 loops, yarn over and pull through all 4 loops on hook to finish.

items.name: 2dec in 3sc:
entries: Make a standard decrease. Starting in the same stitch where your decrease just ended, make another decrease. This results in 2 stitches (2dec) where there were 3sc.

items.name: Half-Triple Crochet (htr):
entries: Yarn over twice, insert hook into stitch, pull up a loop, pull through 2 loops on the hook, yarn over, pull through remaining 3 loops.

notes: This pattern is made in a consistent yarn weight but can be sized up or down by using different weight yarns and hook sizes. Heavier yarn weights will require stronger wire for support.

items: The gap that is visible in the elbow will be closed during assembly. Take 2 pieces of 16-in. / 41-cm wire and fold the edges in so it is not sharp and won't poke through your work. Insert 1 piece down the length of the Finger and the other into the Thumb. Use tape to hold the wires together and continue working the rest of the Arm in a continuous spiral. The untaped end will be inserted into the Body to support the Wing. Lightly stuff the next part of the Arm around the wire as you go. Do NOT stuff the Finger or Thumb.
Fasten off, leaving an extra long yarn tail to sew the Wing Panels to the Wing Supports.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Pin the Beak to the Head. Rnd 1 should be attached at Rnd 9 of the Head, positioned between the eyes. Leave the sides of the Beak around Rows 5–6 unattached so they buckle up to make nostrils. Then attach Rows 7–19 to make the Dragon's Muzzle and pointed upper lip. See images below for reference. Weave in the end once the Beak is attached.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Pin each Eye Ridge over the top of each eye, paying close attention to the position and your Dragon's expression. Once you are happy with the placement, sew each Eye Ridge into place and weave in your ends. The Horns curve from the front point of the Eye Ridge and up the Head. Pin in place to check the positioning, then sew to attach. Sew each Cheek Frill below each Horn, stretching around the Dragon's cheeks / jawline.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Place the back of the Head on the neck, inserting the wire into the Head. Pull the neck flap up and over the Head and sew in place around Rnds 16–18 of the Head. Pin the Head into place before you sew to ensure that you like the positioning. You can add a small amount of fiberfill under the neck flap as you sew it into place, helping to round out the back of the Head.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Position the Back Spikes down the line of your Dragon's spine, starting between the Horns on the Head and ending at the tail. Sew in place when you are happy with them.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Once the Front and Back Legs are complete, pin them in place on the Body and check to make sure your Dragon is stable when standing on its own. Note that the Back Legs are slightly staggered for a more active pose. Once you are happy with the position of the Legs, sew each one to the Body.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: After you've attached the Legs, pin the Belly Scales under the Dragon's jaw, down the chest, between the Front Legs, and ending between the Back Legs. The rows should make a V-shape, pointing downward. When you are happy with the placement, sew the Scales into place.

items.name: Step 1:
entries: For each Wing, there are 3 Wing Panel As, 1 Wing Panel B, and the Wing Support. Join the Wing Panels together. Taking 2 Wing Panel As, place one on top of the other with the sides aligned. Attach your yarn through both pieces at the top point and sc down one long side, joining the pieces together.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: When you get to the end of the 2 pieces, ch1 and flip your piece over. Open the 2 pieces like a book, revealing the backside of the seam you just made. Working into the back of the seam, sc back to the top point. Ch1 and repeat this process to join the third Wing Panel A to your first 2 Wing Panels. Ch1 and prepare to attach Wing Panel B.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: Starting from your ch1 at the top point, sc into both your joined Wing Panel pieces and the longer edge of Wing Panel B. Due to the difference in length, you will reach the end of Panel B first—continue to sc down the unpaired side of your final Panel A piece. Ch1, turn over and sc back up the edge of Panel A and up the back of the seam, ending back at the top point.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: Pin your joined Wing Panels to the bottom of the Wing Support as shown in the image below. When you are happy with the position, use the Wing Support yarn tail to sew the Wing Panels in place. Don't forget to close the gap in the elbow as you go by.

items.name: Step 5:
entries: When your 2 Wings are fully assembled, insert the 2 wire ends (trimmed to about 1½ in. / 38 mm in length) into 2 different stitch spaces at the Dragon's shoulders. Inserting each wire into a different stitch space will help to stabilize the Wing. Pin the Wings in place first to check their position and the overall balance of your Dragon. Once you are happy with the Wing placement, sew the Wing Support opening to the shoulder and weave in your yarn tails.

crochetPattern.name: Schools of Magic Granny Squares
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in WeCrochet {@i Brava} (100% acrylic, 218 yd. / 200 m per 3½ oz. / 100 g skein)
gauge: Gauge is not critical.
notes: When making the Squares, use the tapestry crochet technique, carrying the unused color inside the stitches throughout the row.
All stitches are worked in single crochet.
This pattern can be worked by following the charts for each square or using the written instructions.
finishing: After each granny square is finished, fasten off and weave in all ends. With White, join yarn with a slst to any stitch along the outer edge of the square. Ch 1, and begin working sc evenly around.

To join two squares, place them together with the right side facing inwards and the wrong side facing outwards. Fasten on with new yarn in the corner of the two granny squares. Make a slip stitch using both loops of each stitch on both granny squares, ending at the next corner. Repeat with the remaining squares before creating a white border all around.

entries: Ch2, make scale, sc in middle of next Vstitch, make scale around next Vstitch (2 scales made)

items.name: Row 4:
entries: Ch2, dc in same st, ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in sc, ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in end top corner dc on scale (10)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: Ch2, make scale, [sc in middle of next Vstitch, make scale around next Vstitch] × 2 (3 scales made)

items.name: Row 6:
entries: Ch2, dc in same st, [ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in sc] × 2, ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in end top corner dc on scale (14)

items.name: Row 7:
entries: Ch2, make scale, [sc in middle of next Vstitch, make scale around next Vstitch] × 3 (4 scales made)

items.name: Row 8:
entries: Ch2, dc in same st, [ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in sc] × 3, ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in end top corner dc on scale (18)

items.name: Row 9:
entries: Ch2, make scale, [sc in middle of next Vstitch, make scale around next Vstitch] × 4 (5 scales made)

items.name: Row 10:
entries: Ch2, dc in same st, [ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in sc] × 4, ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in end top corner dc on scale (22)

items.name: Row 11:
entries: Ch2, make scale, [sc in middle of next Vstitch, make scale around next Vstitch] × 5 (6 scales made)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Ch2, dc in same st, [ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in sc] × 5, ch1, 2dc in center of scale, ch1, 2dc in end top corner dc on scale (26)

items: {@i Note: Remember that the ch2 counts as a dc only in the Vstitch rows.}

items.name: Row 13:
entries: Ch2, turn, dc across in each dc (26)

items: Fasten off and weave in all ends. Block the piece to measure approximately 25 in. × 7 in. / 64 cm × 18 cm.
{@i Note: If it's not this exact size, that's fine. You'll just either have a slightly larger or slightly smaller bag.}
{@b (MAKE 2)}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, sc in 2nd ch from hook and across (3)

items: Fasten off and weave in all ends. Block so that each Side Panel measures the same length as the single crochet part of the Body of Bag.

Line up the left Side Panel (right if you are left-handed) with the front of the Bag, pin in place, and sc down the edge of both panels, across the bottom and up the back side. Now surface slst across the back of the pouch top edge, line up the right Side Panel (left if you are left-handed) with the back of the Bag, pin in place, and sc down the edge of both panels, across the bottom and up the front side. Fasten off and weave in all ends.
{@i It helps to use stitch markers or binding clips to keep the Side Panels in place while you attach them to the Bag.}
On the back top edge of the Bag where you slst across, use markers to mark 8 stitches in from each outside edge of the Bag.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Attach yarn in flo at the far right (left for lefties) and sc flo across to the stitch marker (8)

items.name: Row 28:
entries: Ch1, turn, slst through the top of Row 27 as well as the leftover loops at the base of the Belt Loop (8)

items: Fasten off and weave in ends.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Attach yarn in blo of the stitch you marked with the stitch marker, sc across to the edge of the Bag (8)

items.name: Row 28:
entries: Ch1, turn, slst through the top of row 27 as well as the leftover loop at the base of the Belt Loop (8)

items: Fasten off and weave in all ends.
Fold down and line up the flap in the center of the Bag. Mark where the first scale opening is on the Body of the Bag. Sew the button in place where you marked.
